name: hud_ui_composite_panel_entry_constructor_copy_range
description: HudUiCompositePanelEntry::ConstructorCopyRange copies [sourceBegin, sourceEnd) into destination storage by applying the panel ConstructorCopy slice and duplicating the seven trailing flash fields, returning the past-the-end destination pointer.
known_limits: Depends on HudUiPanel::ConstructorCopy, which is functionally accepted but still carries inherited copy/font-clone byte-shape drift.

VC6 cl 12.00.8168 x86 /O2 /Ob0 /Zp4 /FAcs COFF byte comparison for ?ConstructorCopyRange@HudUiCompositePanelEntry@@SIPAU1@PBU1@0PAU1@@Z at 0x4bc320 still fails: 234 unmasked mismatches, 28 relocation bytes masked, 5 trailing VC NOP bytes trimmed, Binary Ninja body is 113 bytes and the VC object symbol is 272 bytes. The drift starts at function entry and includes a larger VC range-loop body.
binary_safe_evidence: Binary Ninja shows a fastcall range loop, sourceBegin in ecx, sourceEnd in edx, destBegin on the stack, a HudUiPanel::ConstructorCopy call for each element, flash field copies at offsets 0x2a4 through 0x2bc, and a past-the-end destination return.
The native composite-entry smoke validates the range return pointer, copied panel fields, copied flash fields, and destination ftable preservation.
